name: Verus — Spec/Proof/Exec IR
summary: Verus's IR has three modes: spec (uncomputable, unrestricted), proof (linear, ghost), and exec (compiled, runs at runtime). The three modes share the same surface syntax but are tracked separately in the IR — the compiler erases spec and proof code before runtime, leaving only exec.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Verus's IR has three modes: `spec` (uncomputable, unrestricted, used in specifications), `proof` (linear, ghost, used in proofs), and `exec` (compiled, runs at runtime). The three modes share the same surface syntax but are tracked separately in the IR — the compiler erases `spec` and `proof` code before runtime, leaving only `exec`.

sections.title: Verification artifacts at zero runtime cost
sections.content:
The contribution: a typed mode system that makes verification artifacts (proofs, specifications) coexist with compiled code in the same IR without affecting runtime cost. Linear ghost permissions in the `proof` mode let ghost code carry capability tokens (e.g., raw-pointer permissions) borrow-checked exactly like real Rust.

This is what makes Verus deployable on production Rust codebases: a `spec` block sits next to ordinary code and contributes nothing to the binary, while a `proof` block discharges obligations that would otherwise need an external verifier with its own copy of the source.

sections.title: Mode example
sections.content:
A three-mode verification IR separates executable code from specification and proof artifacts:

```text
spec function sorted(xs: Seq<Int>) -> Bool

exec function first(xs: Vec<Int>) -> Int
  requires xs.len() > 0
  ensures result == xs[0]
{
  proof { assert(xs.len() > 0); }
  xs[0]
}
```

The `spec` declaration is logical, the `proof` block exists only to discharge obligations, and the `exec` body is what reaches runtime. The generic design lesson is to make erasure explicit in the IR: verification artifacts can be rich without paying runtime cost, provided the compiler tracks which mode each expression belongs to.
